body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The path to `alcomPath` is hardcoded to an absolute path on your local machine. This makes the test non-portable and it will fail on any other developer's machine or in a CI/CD environment. This path should be relative or determined dynamically. Using `import.meta.dir` from Bun is a robust way to achieve this.

```suggestion
const alcomPath = join(import.meta.dir, "../../src/index.ts");

```

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The cleanup logic to remove the temporary directory is commented out. This will cause test runs to leave garbage directories on the file system. This should be re-enabled to ensure tests are clean and don't consume disk space over time.

```typescript
        await rm(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

There is some commented-out code here. It's best to remove dead or commented-out code to keep the codebase clean and avoid confusion for future maintainers.

diff_hunk:
@@ -33,4 +33,31 @@ alcom undo
 alcom finish "feat: 新機能の実装完了"
 ```
 
+### 履歴の確認
+
+```bash
+$ alcom log
+2d2facd --alcom-- test prefix
+...
+```
+
+### Gitコマンドの実行（パススルー）
+
+`alcom git` を使用して、任意のGitコマンドを実行できます。`@base` はセッション開始時のコミットハッシュに置換されます。
+
+```bash
+alcom git diff --stat @base
+```
+
+### Claude Code Hook Example
+
+Claude Codeの `user_prompt_submit` フックなどで使用するスクリプト例です。プロンプトの冒頭30文字をコミットメッセージとして保存します。
+
+```bash
+#!/bin/bash
+input=$(cat)
+prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r .prompt | sed -E 's/^(.{30}).*$/\\1/')
body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `sed` command in this example script appears to have an extra backslash in the replacement part. In a bash script, `\1` is used for back-references, not `\\1`. The double backslash would likely be treated as a literal backslash followed by the digit '1', which is not the intended behavior of capturing and replacing the first 30 characters.

```suggestion
prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r .prompt | sed -E 's/^(.{30}).*$/\1/')

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `isAllowed()` check is repeated in every command's action handler. To reduce code duplication and centralize this logic, you could use `commander`'s `hook` feature. For example, `program.hook('preAction', ...)` could perform this check for all commands that require it. This would make the code cleaner and easier to maintain.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Error messages are being logged to `stdout` using `console.log`. For consistency with other commands like `auto-squash` and `log`, and to follow standard CLI practices, it's better to log error messages to `stderr` using `console.error`.

```suggestion
            console.error(JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', message: error.message }));

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The error logging for this command uses `console.error`, which is great. However, other commands like `save`, `undo`, and `finish` use `console.log` to output the error JSON. It's best to be consistent. Using `console.error` and writing to `stderr` is the standard practice for error messages. I'd recommend changing the other commands to use `console.error` as well for consistency.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `log` command has both `--number` and `--manual-depth` options. However, when `--manual-depth` is used, the `--number` limit is ignored. This interaction isn't documented and might be confusing for users. It would be good to either make them work together (e.g., apply the limit to the result of the `manual-depth` filter) or clearly document in the help text that `--manual-depth` overrides `--number`.

diff_hunk:
@@ -79,3 +79,42 @@ alcom status
 ```bash
 alcom diff
 ```
+
+### `log`
+
+直近のコミット履歴を表示します。デフォルトでは `alcom save` による自動保存コミットのみを表示します。
+
+```bash
+alcom log [options]
+```
+
+- **オプション**:
+    - `-n, --number <count>`: 表示するコミット数（デフォルト: 10）
+    - `-a, --all`: すべてのコミットを表示（手動コミットも含む）
+    - `--manual-depth <count>`: 直近のN個の手動コミットまでの履歴を表示
+
+**例**:
+
+```bash
+$ alcom log
+2d2facd test prefix
+...
+
+$ alcom log --all
+7fbc848 refactor: update docs and index
+2d2facd --alcom-- test prefix
+...
+```
+
+## 活用例
+
+### Claude Code Hook
+
+Claude Codeの `user_prompt_submit` フックなどで使用するスクリプト例です。プロンプトの冒頭30文字をコミットメッセージとして保存します。
+
+```bash
+#!/bin/bash
+input=$(cat)
+prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r .prompt | sed -E 's/^(.{30}).*$/\\1/')
body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Similar to the `README.md`, the `sed` command here seems to have an incorrect back-reference. It should be `\1` instead of `\\1` to correctly substitute the captured group.

```suggestion
prompt=$(echo "$input" | jq -r .prompt | sed -E 's/^(.{30}).*$/\1/')

```

body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The example output for `alcom log` is missing the `--alcom--` prefix in the commit message. The `README.md` example shows `2d2facd --alcom-- test prefix`, which seems correct as it helps distinguish `alcom`'s automated commits. For consistency and clarity, it would be best if this example also included the prefix.

```suggestion
2d2facd --alcom-- test prefix

```
